mission: Build a practical Stephanos VR analysis workspace that turns flat-to-VR research into reusable conversion patterns with Starfield as a primary target.
overview: This lab captures engine, camera, rendering, input, HUD, and performance findings for seated-first VR conversion work.
Cockpit Mode is the pragmatic default: head-tracked seated play with low-friction control mapping and comfort-first frame pacing.
Use this surface as the operational front door while the repo folders hold durable notes, experiments, and tools.

targets.name: Starfield
targets.mode: Cockpit Mode first, then assisted VR
targets.focus: Camera control seams, stereo viability, HUD adaptation, input remapping.

targets.name: Red Dead Redemption 2
targets.focus: Rendering pipeline constraints and reusable module extraction.

knowledgeBuckets: VR Mode Type (Cockpit, Assisted, Motion Control Viability)

techniques.name: Camera Injection
techniques.goal: Stabilize head-tracked seated camera behavior without breaking core gameplay loops.

techniques.name: Stereo Rendering
techniques.goal: Assess depth reconstruction and stereo strategies for non-native VR pipelines.

techniques.name: Tooling Integration
techniques.goal: Standardize capture workflows with RenderDoc/PIX/Ghidra-class tooling.

experiments.name: Camera latency + comfort baseline
experiments.hypothesis: Seated camera injection can stay comfortable if rotational latency and snap controls are bounded.

experiments.name: Stereo compatibility triage
experiments.hypothesis: Deferred render stages can be selectively bypassed or patched to reduce stereo conflicts.

experiments.name: Frame pacing guardrails
experiments.hypothesis: Technique-level constraints can be codified into reusable performance heuristics for conversion projects.

nextActions: Capture initial Starfield research brief in VR-Research-Lab/examples/starfield-vr/README.md.
Add first experiment note under docs/experiment-logs/ with timestamped findings.
Run engine-scanner prototype against target code snapshots and commit reports.
Promote stable findings into module folders (camera-injection, stereo-rendering, input-hooks).
integrationSeams: Memory seam: findings can be serialized as memory candidates for future retrieval indexing.
Experiment seam: docs/experiment-logs and experiments/* are canonical drop points for run artifacts.
Pattern seam: modules/* folders are the reusable output contract for flat-to-VR techniques.
Game-lab seam: examples/starfield-vr and examples/rdr2-vr can evolve into game-specific research tracks.
